title: Patterson's Portuguese Resurrection
title_candidates: From the Wreckage: 49 Points at Praia D'El Rey
The Second-Best Round in TEG History
theme: John Patterson, three holes from oblivion after bleeding eight shots at 4-6, instead detonates a 49-point round — the second-best in TEG history — and converts a one-point deficit into a ten-point Trophy lead.
opening_hook: Open at the 6th green, where Patterson has just signed for back-to-back triple bogeys and looks cooked — then pivot to the scoreboard at sundown showing 49 points beside his name. The whole round bends around this contrast, so theme_led is justified.
foreshadow: Patterson's grim 4-6 stretch (bled 8 shots, three bogeys then two triples) reads like a man surrendering — sets up the birdie at the 7th that stops the rot and the eruption that follows.
Jon Baker cruising through 11-13 in three straight pars (the Trophy leader looking serene) — pays off at the 14th, where the steady run dies in a quadruple bogey alongside Alex Baker.
David Mullin's own bogey-bogey-bogey-bogey march through 6-9 — a quieter bleed that the birdie at the 10th halts, but not before the Trophy race has been wrenched away.

competition_state.name: Trophy
competition_state.note: Wholesale upheaval. Jon Baker began the day a point clear; Patterson took outright lead at the 1st, surrendered it back when Mullin drew level at the 2nd, then briefly looked like a busted flush across holes 4-6. The 49-point round (capped by 17 points across 9-13 and 14 more over 15-18) blew the field apart: a one-point race is now a ten-point chasm, with Mullin, Alex Baker and Jon Baker bunched behind.

competition_state.name: Green Jacket
competition_state.note: Lead held and meaningfully extended. Mullin was two clear of Jon Baker overnight; despite his own mid-round wobble (four straight bogeys, 6-9), the birdie at the 10th steadied him while Jon Baker's quadruple at the 14th did the damage at the other end. Gap out from 2 to 7.

competition_state.name: Wooden Spoon
competition_state.note: Neumann remains rooted to the bottom and the gap has grown from 8 to 14. Quadruple bogeys at the 5th and the 9th, plus a three-hole bleed of nine shots across 11-13, mean the Spoon race is becoming a procession.

players.arc: Bled eight shots across 4-6 and looked finished; the birdie at the 7th stopped the rot, then 17 points across 9-13 and 14 more over 15-18 produced 49 points — the second-best round in TEG history — and turned a one-point Trophy deficit into a ten-point lead.

Retained the Green Jacket lead and extended it from 2 to 7, surviving four consecutive bogeys 6-9 with a steadying birdie at the 10th; briefly drew level for the Trophy at the 2nd before Patterson's onslaught left him 10 adrift.

Started the day as Trophy leader, looked unflappable with three straight pars at 11-13, then watched it unravel with a quadruple bogey at the 14th; rallied for a birdie at the 17th but slipped to joint third in the Trophy and lost ground in the Jacket.

A personal-best 44 points — remarkable given two quadruple bogeys (the 10th and the 14th) and an eight-shot bleed across 10-12 — built on a hot 10-point burst at 7-9; climbs into the Trophy chasing pack.
A quiet round defined by a sextuple-bogey 10 at the 9th; drifts further off the pace in both Trophy and Jacket without ever threatening the leaderboard.
Two quadruple bogeys (5th and 9th) and a nine-shot bleed across 11-13 anchor him firmly at the foot of the Spoon race, the gap to safety doubling from 8 to 14.

venue_notes: TEG's third visit to Praia D'El Rey and to the Lisbon Coast (2016); Cabell B. Robinson's links-parkland hybrid, with the dramatic coastal-dune back nine doing most of the day's heavy lifting — Patterson's eruption began at the 9th and ran through the dunes, while the par-3 14th claimed both Bakers for matching quadruples.
